message.text: Lambda environment variables should not contain secrets: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: environment
Lambda function should have tracing (X-Ray) enabled: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: tracing_config.mode
Lambda function this: no explicit aws_cloudwatch_log_group declared. Lambda will auto-create one with default retention (never expires) and AES256 encryption — no CMK, no controlled retention.
DynamoDB table this: point-in-time recovery (PITR) is disabled. Accidental writes/deletes are not recoverable to within-minute precision.
DynamoDB table this: server-side encryption is not explicitly enabled with a customer-managed KMS key. AWS-owned default encryption is always on, but lacks customer audit control.
DynamoDB table this: deletion_protection_enabled is false. An accidental terraform destroy or API call will permanently delete the table.
Secrets Manager secret this: verify a rotation schedule is configured (aws_secretsmanager_secret_rotation with rotation_rules.automatically_after_days <= 90).
ECR repository this: scan_on_push is not enabled. Pushed images are not automatically scanned for OS-package CVEs.
ECR repository this: image tags are mutable. The same tag (e.g., `latest`, `v1.2.3`) can point at different image SHAs over time — supply-chain attacks become invisible.
EFS file system shared: encrypted = false. Files at rest are not encrypted by KMS.
EFS file system has no AWS Backup policy companion: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: creation_token
EFS file system uses AWS-managed key (not CMK) for encryption: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: creation_token
EventBridge rule scan_ami: no aws_lambda_permission references this rule's arn. If a target is a Lambda, invocations silently 403 — events are dropped without diagnostics.
SNS topic uses AWS-managed key (not CMK) for encryption: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

SQS queue has no dead-letter queue and no customer-managed KMS key: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

SQS queue dlq: no redrive_policy (no dead-letter queue) and no kms_master_key_id. Messages that fail repeatedly are dropped after the retention window, and at-rest data uses the default SQS-managed key (SSE-SQS) rather than a customer-managed CMK you control and audit. (Already a DLQ? Then the missing redrive_policy is expected.)

(Already a DLQ? Then the missing redrive_policy is expected.)

SQS queue has no DLQ — failed messages drop silently: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name
SQS queue dlq: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.
SQS queue this: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.
SQS queue failure: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.

SQS visibility_timeout shorter than consumer Lambda's worst-case run: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

Lambda this: Function URL has authorization_type = NONE with no CloudFront distribution or WAF Web ACL associated. The URL is internet-callable with zero auth and zero rate limiting. Bots will find it within hours of deploy.

Lambda this: no aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm references this function. Errors land in CloudWatch but nobody is paged. The next time this Lambda starts throwing 500s, the on-call learns about it from a customer ticket.

Lambda this: no alarm on Throttles. When this function gets throttled (own cap or account cap), invocations fail silently from the caller's perspective — neither logs nor metrics-as-configured surface it as a paging signal.
SQS queue has no alarm on ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

SQS queue dlq: no alarms reference this queue. Consumer death is invisible from the queue side until messages back up. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage exceeding a few minutes is unambiguous evidence the consumer stopped processing.

SQS queue this: no alarms reference this queue. Consumer death is invisible from the queue side until messages back up. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage exceeding a few minutes is unambiguous evidence the consumer stopped processing.

SQS queue failure: no alarms reference this queue. Consumer death is invisible from the queue side until messages back up. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage exceeding a few minutes is unambiguous evidence the consumer stopped processing.

SQS queue dlq: no alarms reference this queue. Consumer death is invisible from the queue side until messages back up. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage exceeding a few minutes is unambiguous evidence the consumer stopped processing.

SQS queue dlq_us_east_1: no alarms reference this queue. Consumer death is invisible from the queue side until messages back up. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage exceeding a few minutes is unambiguous evidence the consumer stopped processing.

Lambda this: X-Ray tracing is Active but no aws_xray_sampling_rule references this function. At default sampling, high-volume functions produce more trace data than is useful AND bill more than expected.

Lambda this: this plan declares no aws_cloudwatch_dashboard. If incidents are debugged by typing CloudWatch URLs into the AWS console one tab at a time, the next on-call engineer will spend their first 5 minutes rebuilding mental state someone else already had.
SQS queue with delay_seconds > 0 driven by Lambda (SQS-as-scheduler antipattern): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name

SNS topic with delivery_policy retry maximum_retries > 20 (retry storm shape): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name
this: no lifecycle.prevent_destroy block. A terraform destroy or refactor that removes this resource address will delete it without a guardrail.
this: uses `count = condition ? 1 : 0` pattern. Changes to the condition logic can renumber the resource's address, causing terraform to plan a delete+create instead of an update.
codedeploy: uses `count = condition ? 1 : 0` pattern. Changes to the condition logic can renumber the resource's address, causing terraform to plan a delete+create instead of an update.
No aws_inspector2_enabler declared (no vulnerability scanning): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name
this: no aws_inspector2_enabler in the plan. Container / runtime / EC2 CVEs aren't surfaced — the team is doing vulnerability management manually or not at all.

Kinesis stream this: records are not encrypted at rest (encryption_type = NONE or absent). Stream data including PII, financial events, and clickstream records is stored in plaintext on AWS-managed infrastructure.
AWS provider: no default_tags block. Every resource has to tag itself for Cost Explorer — teams forget on a third of them.

A Lambda function with publish = true is being referenced via its unversioned arn. For Lambda@Edge associations, this means edits replicate to all edges before validation against canary traffic.
Messages published to this topic disappear silently — no subscriber receives them.

Secret declared but no resource references it. Pays the per-secret monthly fee for nothing.
Lambda function this: no dead_letter_config. Async-invocation failures retry twice and disappear silently — no signal, no audit trail.
IAM role codedeploy: permissions_boundary unset. Future policy attachments can grant unlimited privilege — the role's effective permissions = union of all attached policies, no ceiling.
